observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: a. cause the immediate submission of certified true copy of all cashbooks duly reconciled with the subsidiary accounting records to the Audit Team for verification; b. assume full responsibility of the Municipal Treasury Office by carrying out the duties and responsibilities provided under Section 470(d) of RA No. 7160, including the overseeing of daily remittance of collections, daily preparation of RCDs, regular scheduling of bank transactions, proper reporting of bank deposits and submission of validated deposit slips to the Municipal Accountant; c. together with the Liquidating Officer, deposit the full amount of ₱15,087,131.57 to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting thereof, accompanied by a satisfactory written justification for failure to deposit the same before the end ofDecember 2023; and d. stop the practice of using cash collections to defray expenses.
observations.reason: The Audit Team did not receive a response from the Municipal Treasurer

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintained and update Subsidiary Ledgers and Cashbooks, respectively, and reconcile the balances on a regular basis in compliance with Section 143 of the LTOM, 2nd Edition, so that discrepancies or errors could be immediately identified and corrected. The Municipal Treasurer to present his cashbook and other relevant records to the examining auditor as provided under Section 348 of RA No. 7160.

We also recommendedthe Municipal Accountant trace the difference between the amount reported by the Municipal Treasury Office and recorded in the books for the month August and September 2024 and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
a. require all Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for verification;
b. direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with accounting records, copy furnish the Audit Team; and
c. designate a Property Officer who shall assist the Municipal Treasurer in maintaining the property cards in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

Additionally, we recommendedtoimplement the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the concern offices (i) revisit the provisions of the aforementioned Circulars; and (ii) ensure compliance with preparation and maintenance of the prescribed forms, registry, and reports related to Semi-Expendable Property

We recommended that the BAC particularly the BAC Secretariat (a) submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2024 either in printed or electronic form, whichever is more convenient or cost-efficient; and (b) henceforth observe regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in such manner and timing as may be deemed reasonable in view of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the:
a. LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Disbursing Officer to immediate submit to the MA the of 132 disbursement vouchers, payrolls, liquidation reports and supporting transaction documentsand submit thereon to the Audit Team pursuant toSection 347 of RA No. 7160and Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 RRSA to avoid Audit Suspensions; and
b. Initiate the necessary administrative sanction in case of unjustified failure/refusal to comply with prompt submission of accounts by said officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board (i) prioritize the formulation of the SEF Budget and submit the same to the Audit Team; and (ii) provide a satisfactory explanation for the failure to prepare and approve the SEF Budget within the prescribed period.

We recommendedthat the BAC (i) prepareand submitthe PMR in prescribed format in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 03-2017; (ii) submit/upload the same in the GPPB website in compliance with Section 12.2 of the R-IRR of RA No. 9184; and (iii) furnish the Audit Team copies of the PMRs for the 1stand 2ndquarter of CY 2024 in hard or electronic copies for review and validation

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (i) prepare and submit Annual Procurement Plan for goods, services and infrastructure projects other than for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment in accordance with 7.1 of RIRR of RA 9184; (ii) exert more diligence in the preparation of APP to ensure the accuracy and completeness of procurement needs based on approved PPMPs and (iii)avail the services of PS-DBM, any procurement that is not made with PS depot shall be supported by certification of non-availability of stock from the PS-DBM.

We recommended that the BAC and Supply Officer (i) procure common-use supplies at the PS-DBM as being required by AO 17, DBM Circular Letter No.2011-6 and IRR of RA 9184; and (ii) in case of non-availability of items in the PS-DBM, the procurement of these common use goods should be procured in bulk quantities to avoid piecemeal purchases.

We recommendedthat the Municipal Accountant provide sufficient, complete and detailed disclosures to the Notes to the Financial Statement pertaining to (i) inventories; (ii) Dormant Accounts; (iii) Local Road Network System; and (iv) DRRMF in accordance with IPSAS and COA Circulars.

We recommended that the:
a. MPDC prepare a monitoring report on the status of implementation of Government PPAs as of December 31, 2024, including all PPAs funded out of the General Fund, 20% Development Fund, current and continuing appropriations, Special Education Fund, Trust Fund and other fund sources; and
b. Municipal Engineer assist the MPDC in monitoring the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality, regardless of fund source.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) coordinate with the BAC to facilitate the submission of the identified lacking documents in accordance with RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; (ii) exercise extra diligence in the performance of pre-audit functions; (iii) be vigilant in the review of the required documents needed for specific transactions; and (iv) be extra cautious in certifying the completeness and propriety of supporting documents of the DVs; and
b. Members of the Bids and awards committee (BAC), Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat (i) submit the basic documentary requirements found lacking in the reviewed contracts to facilitate the conduct of legal, auditorial, and technical review of the aforementioned infrastructure projects; and (ii) strictly observe the provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the:
a. LDRRM Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, ensure the timely submission of the LDRRMFIP and the monthly Report on Utilization ofDRMMFund to the Audit Team;
b. Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) to hire the necessary qualified personnels under the LDRRM Office in compliance with Section 12 of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 04, 2014; and
c. LDRRM Officer secure fidelity bond from the Bureau of Treasury in accordance to Section 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRMO and the concern offices, (i) prepare the required investment plan for the LDRRMF-STF corresponding to the actual expenditures incurred in CY 2024 totaling ₱472,263.57 and should be accompanied with a written justification as to why it was not prepared prior to the disbursement of the fund; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that all the proposed programs, projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF are duly incorporated in the annual LDRRMFIP and integrated in the approved AIP of the Municipality; and
b. MPDC, in coordination with the LDC and upon preparation of the required investment plan by the MDRRMO, prepare a Supplemental AIP and submit it along with the MDRRMO’s justification to the Sangguniang Bayan for their consideration and approval.

We also recommended to ensure that all disbursements charged to the LDRRMF support the disaster risk management programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the: a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to analyzed and reviewed every liabilities and available cash and cash equivalents under the Trust Fund, reflected the outstanding balance of Due to NGAs account. b. ensure that Trust Fund are used exclusively to the purpose/s for which they were granted following the provisions of Section 4(3) of PD No. 1445 and Sections 305(e) and 309(b) of RA No. 7160; and c. adopt cost-saving / economic measures to avoid cash deficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant analyzed and reviewed every accounts in the TF, updated records, and reflected the outstanding balance of Due to NGAs account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a. send demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cashadvances as of December 31, 2023 except those who have already rendered full settlement thereof in CY 2024; b. cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who still failed to render full settlement of cash advances despite the issuance of demand letter pursuant to Section5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and c. henceforth, (i) refrain from allowing additional cash advances to any accountable officers who have not yet fully settled their previous cash advances and (ii) strictly impose the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.97-002 in order to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Accountable officers are instructed to liquidate cash advances on a weekly basis

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the Real Property Tax Registers or Taxpayer’s index card are updated and regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year at the beginning of the year to serve as basis in establishing the accurate Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables in compliance with Section 4. Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started the preparation of taxpayers database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant (i) transfer the road networks carried in the registries of the Public Infrastructure to the books of accounts of the LGU by preparing the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively; (ii) support the JEV with the Registry of Public Infrastructure for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with the same and non-availability of cost per registry; (iii) at the end of each year, a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each depreciable road component must be prepared as basis for recording depreciation expenses and the required Local Road Network Ledger Card for each road network shall likewise be maintained; (iv) record depreciation in the books; and (iv) provide disclosure on the Local Road Networks of the LGU in the Notes to the Financial Statements; b. Municipal Mayor create/update the inventory committee consisting of representatives from different concerned offices to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality who will prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System (RPCRNS) using the form in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008. The RPCRNS should be reconciled with the Accounting and GSO records; c. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer/GSO with the complete description and segregation of road components for those with data on construction cost. For those not posted in the registry but without identified cost and those included in the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System but not in the registry, the fair value must be used in determining the cost of road components and thereafter shall provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and d. Supply Officer/General Services Officer, at the end of every accounting period, render a Report on Local Road Network using the pro-forma schedule in Annex A of same Circular and maintain local road inventory, Road Map and complete Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started the transfer of road registries to the books.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Disbursing Officer exert joint efforts to cause the immediate submission of disbursement vouchers, payrolls, liquidation reports and supporting transaction documents to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 347 of RA No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 RRSA; and Municipal Mayor initiate the necessary administrative and/or criminal action in case of unjustified failure/refusal to comply with prompt submission of accounts by subordinate officials.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Disbursing Officer exert joint efforts to cause the immediate submission of disbursement vouchers, payrolls, liquidation reports and supporting transaction documents to the Audit Team; Municipal Mayor initiated the necessary administrative action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (i) prepare and submit Annual Procurement Plan for goods, services and infrastructure projects other than for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment in accordance with 7.1 of R-IRR of RA 9184; (ii) exert more diligence in the preparation of APP to ensure the accuracy and completeness of procurement needs based on approved PPMPs and (iii) ensure that the items not available in PS-DBM is well supported by certification from the PS-DBM regarding stocks availability; and BAC and Supply Officer (i) procure common-use supplies at the PS-DBM as being required by AO 17, DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 and IRR of RA 9184; and (ii) in case of non-availability of items in the PS-DBM, the procurement of these common use goods should be procured in bulk quantities to avoid piecemeal purchases.
observations.managementAction: The BAC prepared procurement list for common-use supplies only; BAC & Treasury to check price and quality and prioritize PS-DBM. Secure certification for non-availability when necessary.
observations.reason: Similar audit observations were noted in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) for CY 2021-2030 be prepared by the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to serve as the guide in the implementation of the Municipality’s development plans for the current year and the years ahead. We also recommended that the CLUP be prepared for a ten-year planning period at the minimum, and annual review be conducted to ensure attainment of desired development objectives. In the preparation of the CLUP, strict adherence to guidelines of the HLURB is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and concerned offices gathered data and prepared draft CLUP; CLUP preparation initiated.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and insufficient fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC prepare a monitoring report on the status of implementation of Government PPAs as of December 31, 2023, including all PPAs funded out of the General Fund, 20% Development Fund, current and continuing appropriations, Special Education Fund, Trust Fund and other fund sources; and Municipal Engineer assist the MPDC in monitoring the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality, regardless of fund source.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC prepared and submitted monitoring reports to the Municipal Accountant; Municipal Engineer assisted and provided updated status of implementation.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in implementing offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Local Development Council and the MPDC, together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee (i) immediately look into the unimplemented projects under the 20% DF and determine the causes of their non- implementation; (ii) formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects; (iii) consider formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects; and (iv) review the costing of projects programmed two to eight years ago to conform to the present conditions; b. The Municipal Engineer (i) improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of the Program of Work of unimplemented projects; and (ii) ensure that plans are accurate and projects estimates are based on standard and realistic amounts; c. The MPDC (i) enhance monitoring of project implementation in coordination with the implementing offices; and (ii) submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund; and d. Municipal Mayor (i) ensure the full or optimum utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with Section 5 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits of the intended beneficiaries; (ii) provide additional manpower in the Office of the MPDC and ME to assist them in implementing and monitoring development projects; and (iii) convene MPDC, ME and other offices department heads concerned to strongly pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries and remind them of their duties and responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: Local Development Council and MPDC coordinated with implementing offices; ME improved planning and scheduling; MPDC enhanced monitoring and submitted status reports; LCE issued memorandum to expedite implementation and to consider early biddings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO, in close coordination with the MPDC, review the planned programs and intensity monitoring and formulate strategies that will ensure timely implementation thereof.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO and MDRRMC conducted meetings and prepared plans to speed up implementation and utilization of disaster related PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: a. Require the HRMO to formulate a Human Resource Staffing Pattern Plan in accordance to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 series of 2022 dated November 8, 2022 and issue a uniform policy guideline on the hiring thereof as well as to strengthen the internal controls pertaining thereto; b. Prioritize and consider filling up the vacant plantilla positions prior hiring of JO and COS personnel that are based on the uniform policy guide-lines; and c. Evaluate and determine the appropriate personnel complement of each office/department in the Municipal Government and effect the necessary amendments in their respective plantilla, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Management prepared a staffing pattern plan and started filling up vacant plantilla positions; some reclassifications were made and vacancies advertised.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. GFPS Executive Committee: i. continue in promoting and ensuring gender mainstreaming in local policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, implementation, monitoring and evaluation; ii. submit to the AT hard copy of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report duly received by the DILG; iii. set GAD Agenda to be addressed by the LGU during the three-year term of its leadership in accordance with the guidelines for preparation set forth by PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2018-04 dated September 19, 2018; and iv. periodically (i) review, evaluate and recalibrate, as necessary, its GAD code to keep up with changes and development in addressing gender issues to ensure that the outcome of MGA’s GAD system remains in line with the government in achieving its GAD objectives; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) in accordance with PCW MC No. 2009-1 dated September 16, 2009 and Section 105 of MGA’s GAD Code; and b. MPDC, in coordination with the GFPS-TWG, complete the establishment of a GAD database containing the gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data of the Municipality in accordance with Section 37-D, Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9710 and henceforth keep it updated such that it may serve as a reliable basis for addressing gender issues and concern in GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: GFPS reconstituted and strengthened; GAD Plan and Budget prepared and submitted; GAD Accomplishment Report prepared and submitted; GAD database work initiated and MPDC to seek technical assistance for GAD database establishment.
observations.reason: Some components (GAD Agenda, IRR, database) still require further development; lack of technical assistance and personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (i) submit a reasonable explanation for failure to deposit the cash collections totaling ₱10,978,572.06; (ii) deposit all the cash in local treasury to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting thereof; (iii) assume full responsibility of the Municipal Treasury Office by carrying out the duties and responsibilities provided under Section 470.d of RA No. 7160, including overseeing the daily remittance of collections, daily preparation of RCDs, regular scheduling of bank transactions, proper reporting of bank deposits and submission of validated deposit slips to the Municipal Accountant; and (iv) cause the immediate submission of a certified true copy of all cashbooks maintained by accountable officers in the Treasury Office.
The Municipal Treasurer committed to update all cashbooks and submit a certified copy thereof to the Audit Team on September 30, 2022. He also committed to deposit all cash collections as soon as the possible.
The Municipal Mayor assured that she will see to it that the Municipal Treasurer will perform the audit recommendations mentioned.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) exert more efforts to finish updating the subsidiary ledgers for Construction in Progress and thereafter, prepare the accounting entries to record the completed infrastructures in their respective accounts, including the corresponding allowances for depreciation; (ii) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, prepare a comprehensive report on the correct valuation of all infrastructure assets of the Municipality and record adjustments in the books of accounts as needed; (iii) henceforth, ensure that subsidiary ledgers for all infrastructure asset and construction in progress accounts are regularly updated;
Municipal Treasurer, as Property and Supply Officer in the absence of a GSO, (i) prepare a detailed property record for the municipal infrastructure assets based on the report of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer; and (ii) ensure that henceforth, such property record is regularly updated and reconciled with the accounting records; and
Municipal Mayor enjoin all concerned department heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for the Local Roads Asset Management System as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
The Accountant and the Engineer agreed to jointly put effort in complying with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the immediate preparation and submission of a complete set of the general and subsidiary ledgers on the fund transfers from NGAs covering the period January 1 to December 31, 2021, and henceforth ensure that they are regularly updated and that their balances tally with the amounts reflected on the monthly financial reports.
The Accountant said that she is currently updating the general and subsidiary ledgers on the fund transfers from NGAs and assured that the said reports will be submitted to the Audit Team as soon as possible.

We recommended that the Presiding Officer and Members of the SB review and consider amending the provisions of Ordinance No. 57, particularly those that are inconsistent with AO No. 402, s.1998, and its implementing rules and regulations.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the submission of the complete supporting documents on the reimbursement of expenses for medical check-up and diagnostic procedures totaling ₱18,589.88, and henceforth ensure that all claims for medical assistance are supported with complete documentation.
The Management said that they will temporarily suspend the implementation of the medical check-up program until the SB has amended the subject ordinance pursuant to AO No. 402, s. 1998.

We recommended that the Management submit a written justification for their use of Ordinance No. 90 as basis for budgetary realignment and disbursement of the amount of ₱205,492.87, instead of an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget that was prepared, authorized and reviewed in accordance with the rules and procedures provided under Chapters 1, 2 and 3, Part II of the BOM for LGUs prescribed under LBC No. 112 dated June 10, 2016.
We recommended further that the Municipal Budget Officer henceforth, ensure that all claims against the local funds have existing and available fund appropriations based on a duly enacted annual or supplemental budget pursuant to Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.
The Municipal Budget Officer and the MPDC agreed with the audit recommendation and promised that they will henceforth follow the rules and procedures for budgetary realignments and supplemental budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the LDC, (a) impose a policy for strict adherence to Section 109 of RA No. 7160 in carrying out their function as mandated thereon to ensure that priority development programs and projects are well-planned, procurement-and-implementation ready, and properly coordinated with the implementing offices and other agencies concerned; and (b) consider realigning the funds for unimplemented projects to other viable priority projects.
We recommended that the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer (a) formulate strategies on how to fast track the implementation of the priority development programs and projects; and (b) intensify the monitoring, inspection and evaluation of its implementation and regularly submit quarterly reports with recommendations for management actions on programs and projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments, copy furnish the Audit Team.
The Management assured that the unimplemented projects in CY 2021 and unutilized funds under the prior years’ 20% Development Fund shall be prioritized in the upcoming LDC meeting for appropriate action. The MPDC also assured that quarterly reports on the status of implementation of programs and projects shall be submitted to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the MDRRMO, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), review the planned programs and projects under the LDDRM Plan, intensify monitoring and formulate strategies that will ensure timely implementation thereof.
The MDRRMO said that the identified projects in the audit observation shall be discussed in the upcoming MDRRMC meeting while several projects were already coordinated with the Bids and Awards Committee to initiate the procurement process. He assured that all programs and projects under LDRRMF shall be implemented and regularly monitored.

We recommended that the MPDC, in coordination with the GFPS-TWG, initiate the establishment of a GAD database containing the gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data of the Municipality in accordance with Section 37-D, Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9710 and henceforth keep it updated such that it may serve as a reliable basis for addressing gender issues and concern in GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing years.
The MPDC said that he already coordinated with the GFPS-TWG and that they have started to prepare the GAD database through the conduct of Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS).

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the:
Municipal Treasurer (i) submit a reasonable explanation for not being able to deposit intact all collections from prior years totaling ₱7,643,330.63; (ii) deposit all the cash under his custody to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury Account; (iii) assume full responsibility of the Municipal Treasury Office by carrying out the duties and responsibilities provided under Section 470.d of RA No. 7160, including overseeing the daily remittance of collections, daily preparation of RCDs, regular scheduling of bank transactions and proper reporting of bank deposits in the RCD, duly supported with validated deposit slips; and (iv) cause the immediate submission of a certified true copy of all cashbooks from January 1, 2013 to the present date; and
Municipal Accountant (i) cause the immediate submission of the cash collection reports and transaction documents for the month of December 2019; and (ii) henceforth, observe prompt submission of reports and supporting documents to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor, through the Inventory Committee, require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; and
Municipal Treasurer prepare Property Cards per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (i) cause the immediate submission of the lacking reports and transaction documents to the Audit Team, considering that the ECQ has already been lifted in the Province of Palawan and replaced by the Modified General Community Quarantine (MGCQ) on June 1, 2020; and (b) henceforth, exert more coordinated efforts in the prompt preparation of RCIs, RDs and the monthly financial reports to ensure timely submission thereof to the Municipal Accountant along with complete transaction documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
issue demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2019 except those who have already rendered full settlement thereof in CY 2020;
cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who still failed to render full settlement of cash advances despite the issuance of demand letter pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
henceforth, (i) refrain from allowing any additional cash advance to accountable officers who have not yet fully settled their previous cash advance and (ii) strictly impose the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer (a) submit a reasonable explanation for signing the checks and releasing payment for DVs and payrolls totaling ₱9,638,925.65 despite lack of certification as to availability of funds and the LCE’s final approval; and (b) henceforth, strengthen internal control over disbursement of fund by ensuring that no disbursement of government funds is made without the complete certification and approval of proper officials pursuant to Section 4.5 of PD No. 1445 and Section 344 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the (a) BAC immediately submit the lacking documents; and (b) Municipal Accountant submit explanation for signing the disbursement voucher despite incomplete supporting documentation.
We recommended that the (a) Municipal Engineer submit justification for failure to detect the errors in the contractor’s SWA and failure to provide the Statement of Time Elapsed, Inspection Report and pictures work items which correspond to 40.44% work accomplishment; and (b) Municipal Accountant submit justification for signing the disbursement voucher despite the deficiencies and an explanation for full recuperation of the contractor’s advance payment.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit (a) the disbursement voucher and supporting documents for Check No. 57515045; and (b) satisfactory justification for not requiring the refund of excess payment amounting to ₱1,207,948.71, along with the latest approved inspection report and certificate of completion; otherwise, issue a demand letter duly approved by the Municipal Mayor, requiring the contractor to refund the excess payment.
We recommended that the (a) Municipal Engineer prepare an updated inspection report, statement of time elapsed and a computation for liquidated damages in accordance with Section 8, Annex “E” of the IRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) Municipal Accountant ensure that the correct amount of liquidated damages as determined by the Municipal Engineer is deducted from any subsequent payment to the contractor.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and MPDC jointly submit justification for processing the disbursements totaling ₱1,150,500.00, despite lack of an investment plan and without conducting the proper procurement process in accordance with RA No. 9184; and
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO), henceforth, prepare a DRRMF Investment Plan which includes the list of projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended balance of prior years’ DRRMF in accordance with the approved Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (MDRRMP), for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that the:
MPDC formulate integrated economic, social, physical and other developmental plans and policies for consideration of the Municipal Development Council in coming up with a comprehensive local development plan that is compliant with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017;
Municipal Development Council exercise prudence in the formulation of the development plans and public investment programs to ensure that the 20% DF is used solely for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and
Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that all disbursements out of the 20% DF are (i) supported with a duly approved Program of Work or other documentation that would establish the nature of the expenditure as investment or capital outlay; and (ii) not among the list of expenditure items which are prohibited under Section 4.0 of the same JMC.

We recommended that the:
MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports thereon with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments;
Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit to the Audit Team a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects; and
Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to ensure prompt delivery of basic services and facilities to the public and achieve the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson (a) submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2019 either in printed or electronic form, whichever is more convenient or cost-efficient; and (b) henceforth observe regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in such manner and timing as may be deemed reasonable in view of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the monitoring reports on the implementation of government PPAs for CY 2019 in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee and the Municipal Accountant: (a) cause the immediate submission of the GPB, GAD AR and other GAD reports and documents requested by the Audit Team for CY 2019; and (b) henceforth, be mindful of the schedule to be observed in GAD planning and budgeting as provided under Section 4.1.C.5 PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, to ensure timely submission of GAD related reports and documents in accordance with Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (i) submit a written explanation on why taxes withheld were not remitted intact and regularly to the BIR; (ii) review their records/schedule of taxes withheld and remitted to determine the causes of negative balances and rule out the possibility of errors in recording; (iii) cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible interests, penalties and surcharges for late payments; and (iv) henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld are remitted intact and regularly within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended; and
Municipal Mayor cause the refund of ₱37,144.22 by concerned local officials who failed to remit taxes withheld to the BIR within the prescribed time.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor (i) identify the local officials or employees who caused unnecessary delays in the remittance of GSIS contributions and loan amortizations and cause the refund of penalty amounting to ₱604.47; and (ii) as the agency head, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to implement and monitor a sound internal control system over GSIS contributions and loan amortizations to ensure timely recording and remittance thereof;
Municipal Accountant (i) prepare an analysis of the unremitted GSIS contributions and loan amortizations, reconcile billings and remittances and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections, as appropriate; and (ii) record transactions promptly as soon as they are reported by the Municipal Treasurer and refrain from recording transactions that did not actually occur to prevent misleading the users of the financial statements; and
Municipal Treasurer, (i) based on the analysis prepared by the Municipal Accountant, remit to the GSIS all contributions and/or loan amortizations from prior periods; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that GSIS contributions and loan amortization are remitted intact within the prescribed period under RA No. 8291 for the welfare of the employees and to avoid incurrence of unnecessary penalties.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor (i) identify the local officers or employees who caused unnecessary delays in the remittance of PhilHealth premium contributions and cause the refund of interests and surcharges totaling ₱1,902.93; and (ii) as the agency head, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to implement and monitor a sound internal control system over PhilHealth contributions and remittances;
Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the overpayment of PhilHealth contributions, reconcile billings and remittances, coordinate with PHIC regarding the recorded receivable of ₱38,473.06 and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate; and
Municipal Treasurer, henceforth, take charge of the prompt remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed by Philippine Health Insurance Corporation to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2018 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.
