observations.reason: The Management failed to allocate at least 1% of its IRA for LCPC for CY 2018. No reason/justification was submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. The LDRRM Officer should submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Local Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Part of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of ₱14,579.98 for CY 2017 should be transferred to the Trust Fund and be deposited to the respective bank account. Follow NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP and the monthly report were not submitted. Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO were informed to submit reports.
observations.reason: Monitoring laxity of the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant. LDRRMFIP and the monthly reports for CY 2017 and CY 2018 have not been submitted to the Audit Team due to the laxity of the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the
Municipal Treasurer:

a. deposit all the cash under his custody to the AGDB and prepare a report
showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury Account and submit a
reasonable explanation for not being able to deposit intact all collections
from prior years;

b. assume full responsibility of the Municipal Treasury Office by carrying out
the duties and responsibilities provided under Section 470.d of RA No. 7160,
including overseeing the (i) daily remittance of collections; (ii) daily
preparation of RCDs; (iii) regular scheduling of bank deposits; and (iv)
proper reporting of bank deposits in the RCD, duly supported with validated
deposit slips; and

c. immediately submit a certified true copy of all his cashbooks for the period
January 1, 2016 up to the present date.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor, through the Inventory Committee, require all the
Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under
their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for
verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; and

b. Municipal Treasurer prepare Property Cards per category of PPE and
henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a. submit (i) a revised SUCA as of December 31, 2018 covering all Advances
accounts in all funds including SEF and TF, with complete and detailed
information on the date granted, due date for liquidation and status of
accountable officer; and (ii) a copy of general and subsidiary ledgers for
Advances accounts under SEF and TF;

b. impose strictly the provision requiring all accountable officers to fully and
promptly liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it
was given has been served or at the end of each year, whichever comes first;

c. cause the issuance of demand letters to all accountable officers who have
unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018 and still failed to
liquidate the same as of September 30, 2019; and

d. henceforth, ensure prompt submission of reports and records pertaining to
cash advances to allow timely review of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

a. inform the Provincial Treasurer that the provision for granting discount on RPT
as provided under the New Revenue Code of Palawan is in conflict with
Section 246 of RA No. 7160, Article 342 of the IRR of RA No. 7160 and
Section 177 of the LTOM;

b. propose to the Provincial Treasurer the issuance of a revised Notice of
Payment for CY 2018 or any other means to disseminate information to
taxpayers, clarifying that RPT payments made during the first quarter of the
year are not considered “advanced” but instead “prompt” and subject only to
10% discount;

c. collect the amount of under-collection of RPT due from taxpayers for CY 2018
but only after providing them clear and sufficient information on the correct
discounts and taxes due, notwithstanding the issuance of a Notice of
Delinquency in accordance with Section 254 of RA No. 7160 and Article 345
of its IRR; and

d. henceforth, adhere to Article 342 of the IRR of RA No. 7160 in granting
discounts on RPT payments.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor:

i. instruct the BAC Secretariat to submit: (i) copy of the BAC Resolution
recommending and justifying the use of alternative mode of procurement
for the Livelihood Development Program, if there is any; and (ii) copy of
the APP and PPMP or excerpts thereof wherein procurement for the
Livelihood Development Program is included; and

ii. through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator: (i) make
proper coordination with BFAR in order to obtain a copy of the approved
WFP and the Official Receipt evidencing the return of unused fund; and
(ii) henceforth, ensure that all fund transfers are supported with complete
documents including the MOA, WFP, approved project proposals,
implementation schedules and specific guidelines;

b. Municipal Accountant henceforth ensure that: (a) all DVs are supported with
complete documents, especially those pertaining to procurement; and (b) all
Fund Utilization Reports are duly supported with the appropriate MOA,
WFP, approved project proposals, implementation schedules and specific
guidelines.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Engineer (i) submit a valid explanation on the delayed completion of
the project and failure to impose liquidated damages; (ii) prepare inspection
report on the current status of the project and the computation of liquidated
damages to be deducted from the next progress billing; and (iii) henceforth,
exercise due diligence in the preparation of Program of Works, Detailed
Engineering Designs and PERT/CPM Analysis for all infrastructure projects,
conduct regular inspection and submit duly certified inspection reports to the
Municipal Mayor, copy furnished the Audit Team;

b. MPDC (i) actively monitor and evaluate the implementation of programs and
projects and submit a quarterly report to the Municipal Mayor, copy
furnished the Audit Team; and (ii) henceforth, whenever designated as
Acting Municipal Engineer, ensure that infrastructure projects are regularly
inspected and issued with duly certified inspection reports; and

c. Municipal Treasurer submit the official receipt issued by the contractor for
the payment of 80.87% progress billing or a valid explanation for the lack
thereof, as the case may be.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor issue a demand letter to RAK Trading and Construction
ordering the refund of ₱252,357.46 for failure to execute the works in
accordance with the Contract Agreement for the Construction of Level II
Water Supply System at Barangay Maracañao, Agutaya, Palawan;

b. Municipal Engineer submit an explanation for failure of the contractor to
implement the project in accordance with the contract agreement and a
narrative report on the actions taken by the Management to address the
deficiency; and

c. Municipal Accountant submit an explanation for allowing the advance
payment amounting to 30% of the contract price despite the incomplete
supporting documents and non-compliance with Annex E of RA 9184.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor (i) communicate to the DILG through the MLGOO the
importance of entering into a MOA for every fund transfer as required
under COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and (ii) submit a
reasonable explanation for issuing NTP prior to the approval of contract;

b. Municipal Engineer submit (i) a summary report of all inspections conducted
on the project, duly supported with duly certified inspection reports,
statements of time elapsed and pictures of work items; (ii) detailed
engineering design for the project with complete plans and drawings; (iii)
duly approved program of works and detailed computation of the approved
budget for the contract; (iv) project implementation schedule and approved
PERT/CPM Network Diagram, and (v) a satisfactory explanation for the
delay in the completion of the project, duly supported with valid
documentation;

c. BAC Chairperson submit a copy of all available documentation on the
procurement activities conducted for the project, including the APP, bidding
documents, minutes of BAC meetings and negotiations, results of mandatory
reviews, bidders’ final quotations, performance security and other related
documents; and

d. Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking supporting documents and
henceforth, ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete
documentation pursuant to Section 4.6 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the:

a. BAC Chairperson submit (i) a valid explanation for non-conduct of public
bidding for the procurement of 450 bags of cement and why the supplier was
changed from BEF Trading to SJFive Trading and Construction; and (ii) a
narrative report on all activities performed by the BAC for the said
procurement, duly supported with complete and valid documentation
including resolutions, minutes of meetings and price quotations, if any;

b. Municipal Accountant henceforth ensure that all claims for payment are
supported with complete documentation, especially those pertaining to the
procurement process; and

c. Municipal Treasurer (i) submit the official receipt issued by the supplier for
the payment of 450 bags of cement, and (ii) henceforth, ensure that cash
collections are not used for payment of municipal expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:

a. MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of
development projects and henceforth submit quarterlyp reports on the same
with recommendations for management actions on projects with
unsatisfactory work accomplishments; and

b. Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal
public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of
all infrastructure projects.

We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Accountant prepare a computation of excess payments made to RX
Caremed Enterprises and deduct the excess amount upon release of the
retention money;

b. The BAC Chairperson cause the immediate submission of complete
documentation on the procurement of drugs and medicines from RX
Caremed Enterprises amounting to ₱489,050.00; and

c. The requesting officer submit certification that the requested drug products
conform with the PNDF, Volume I, current edition, pursuant to Section 2 of
EO No. 49.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the MDRRMO to
prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and
submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on the period
prescribed under the same COA Circular.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the MSWMB cause the
preparation and immediate submission of the revised 10-year solid waste
management plan of the Municipality and coordinate with PCSD, NSWMC and
other government agencies for the establishment of a compliant solid waste disposal
facility.

We recommended that the Municipal GAD Focal Person:

a. submit a reasonable explanation for the late submission of the CY 2018 GPB
to the DILG;

b. submit valid documentation that can support the results of the gender
responsiveness checklists and justify the perfect scores given to the 12
attributed programs totaling ₱1,208,386.00; and

c. henceforth, submit promptly the Municipal GPB on or before March 31 of
each year to ensure timely review and endorsement of the same by the DILG.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to analyze the
unremitted balance of the Due to PhilHealth account, reconcile billings and
remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the remittance
of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed under PhilHealth Circular
No. 0001, s. 2014 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the unremitted
balance of the Due to GSIS account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect
necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the remittance
of GSIS contributions within the period prescribed under RA No. 8291 to avoid
incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of taxes
withheld to the BIR within the prescribed period to avoid possible interest/penalty
for late payments and/or possible misuse of the government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission
of complete financial reports and supporting schedules including the financial
transaction documents as required under Section 2.0 of COA Circular 2010-001
dated March 2, 2010.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (a) direct the
Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all
management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations
included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2017 using the prescribed AAPSI
Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the
submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties and to prepare the inventory report using the prescribed format for RPCPPE. The Inventory Committee should also check the condition of the property, any damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and immediately request for disposal.
observations.managementAction: According to the Management, they are reorganizing the members of the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the necessary reports.
observations.reason: No justification/comment was offered by the management. Reiterated in Finding No. 2.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer - Designate to deposit immediately all the cash collections under their custody with the authorized government depository bank, in compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, they should submit an explanation letter for not depositing the collections intact and promptly. We also reiterated for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile the balances of cash accounts per Cashbook and General Ledger thus, resulted to unreliable and doubtful account balances.
observations.managementAction: According to the Management, they are reorganizing the members of the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the necessary reports. (Note: Management response regarding cash recommendation not clearly provided.)
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer has not yet deposited the undeposited collections and failed to submit an explanation letter as required in the audit recommendation. Reiterated in Finding No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to follow the prescribed guidelines and procedures pertaining to Petty Cash Fund as stated under Sections 48 and 52 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that the prescribed procedures to Petty Cash Fund were followed in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management should send Demand Letters to those Accountable Officers to immediately settle their cash advances with due recourse to the consequences that may be instituted as enumerated above. We also recommended for the Management to stop the practice of granting advance payments or personal cash advances to officials and employees against their salaries and allowances for a particular period which the services are not yet rendered. Likewise, we reiterated that the management should make other appropriate action such as, but not limited to, institutions of sanctions as provided for under Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Henceforth, we recommended for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit the revised SUCA and the copy of general and subsidiary ledgers for Advances accounts. (Management agreed to implement recommendations in 2019.)
observations.reason: No report on actions taken provided in the matrix. Reiterated in Finding No. 3.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Office of the Municipal Accountant periodically reconcile the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds, Due to Other Funds and other reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary adjustments should be effected in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant periodically reconciled the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Monitoring report as of December 31, 2017 was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management procure claims for telephone/communications services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance with the provisions of Appendix 13 of the IRR of the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184). The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to require that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be supported with Statements of Account/Bill from telecommunication service providers, official receipts/ invoices and certification that calls are official as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor ensured that claims for telephone/communication services for cellular/mobile phones were allowed by management in CY 2018. The Municipal Accountant ensured that the prescribed procedures to Petty Cash Fund were followed in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and/or the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring report as of December 31, 2017 was submitted to the Audit Team. Henceforth, management to strictly comply with the provisions.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should maintain the accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the GPPB and to this Office within 14 days at the end of each semester. The BAC should invite COA representative as Observer in its procurement process especially during eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination of bids, bid evaluation, opening of the bids, and post-qualification. Likewise, the BAC should see to it that at least one observer must come from a duly recognized group in a sector and the other from a non-government organization (NGO). The Municipality should require their bidders to submit Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the HOPE or to public officers or employees by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree and the same should be submitted to the Audit Team together with the required documentary requirements. Reconstitute BAC composition to comply with Section 11.2.3 of R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184. The Municipality should procure its common-use supplies, materials and equipment under DBM-PS or secure a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) before procuring from other sources.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the Municipality observed the procedures on procurement, Reiterated in Finding Nos. 5, 7, 8 and 9. The Municipality through its Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), invited observers in its procurement process and required its bidders to submit Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the HOPE. Moreover, the Municipality reconstituted its BAC membership.
observations.reason: PMR for CY 2017 and 2018 were still not submitted to the Audit Team and supporting documents were still not submitted to the Audit Team. Moreover, the Management failed to reconstitute its BAC officers by composition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and immediately submit to the Audit Team the copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents which are due on the prescribed period to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was requested to submit copies of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Copies of contracts and purchase orders were still not submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality should pay for the delivered items and accomplished projects only. Thus, it should avoid the advance payment on government contracts. We recommended for the Municipality to enhance transparency in the procurement especially by posting its procurement opportunities in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Municipality, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for the purpose. The Municipality should require submission of documentary requirements such as Technical and Financial Documents as enumerated under Section 23.1 of the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Likewise, the BAC should award only the contract to the eligible supplier only. Disbursement Vouchers must conform with the proper procedures such as the pre-requisites of gathering of the supporting documents and approved Obligation Requests.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the Municipality paid for delivered items and management observed the procedures on procurement, payment and preparation of documents. The Municipality coordinated to require bidders to submit Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to HOPE.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were still not submitted to the Audit Team. Reiterated in Finding Nos. 5, 7, 8 and 9.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of all the programmed projects and activities charged to 20% DF during the year and previous years to achieve effectively and efficiently its socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and the Municipal Engineer were required to properly monitor project implementation. Management to intensify monitoring and evaluation.
observations.reason: Failure of the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to properly monitor implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipality to faithfully comply with the preparation and submission of the SEF documents as prescribed under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. We recommended that the disbursement to be made out of the SEF must be within the budget in accordance with Section 100(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 4 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. Lastly, we recommended the Municipality, through its Municipal Treasurer, to exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real property for the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: Documents and accounts were to be submitted; the Municipal Treasurer instructed to deposit cash under his custody. (Specific management action not fully documented.)
observations.reason: Documents and reports required for the review of SEF accounts were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that Management coordinate with concerned agencies for the prompt approval and implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality. We also recommended the management to initiate the immediate establishment of Barangay Materials Recovery Facility. The Municipal Mayor to make necessary corrective measures and devise a way to conform to the mentioned regulations. Henceforth, strictly comply with provisions of RA No. 9003 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, Management coordinated with concerned agencies for the approval. The Materials Recovery Facility has been established on the Barangays. The revised solid waste management plan has not been submitted for approval due to non-completion of WACS.
observations.reason: Revised solid waste management plan has not been submitted to the concerned agencies for approval due to non-completion of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS).

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Management to strictly comply with Section 48 of the General Provisions of R.A. No. 10964 known as the General Appropriations Act of FY 2018 for the monthly net take home pay of the regular employees for CY 2018 which is not lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00), after all authorized deductions based on the above order of preference presented.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured compliance with Section 48 of the GAA FY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Management to deduct and remit the PhilHealth contributions as prescribed by PhilHealth Circular No. 2017-0024 dated September 11, 2017, for CY 2018. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the said contributions on the prescribed period to avoid interest and surcharge, otherwise issue Notice of Charge for the interests and surcharges incurred for delayed remittance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipality deducted the correct amount of contributions and the Municipality’s officers and employees were charged accordingly. Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile and deduct where necessary.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still failed to remit the correct amount of contributions on the prescribed period. Reiterated in Finding No. 15.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant for the immediate remittance of taxes withheld (all funds) to the BIR. We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to adjust, trace and resolve the discrepancy on the Due to BIR account balance and ensure that all transactions are properly recorded. The Municipal Treasurer to ensure availability of accountable forms and that supporting documents printed on thermal paper be photocopied.
observations.managementAction: Management was requested to immediately remit and reconcile withholding taxes; adjustments were to be made by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Trust Fund checks were used for payment resulting to overstatement of Due to BIR account. Some supporting documents were not submitted. Reiterated in Finding No. 17.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the aforecited regulations. We also recommend the immediate submission of the CY 2016 copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended the Municipality to establish LCPC and allocate at least 1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to ensure implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Inventory
Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties and prepare
the inventory report using the prescribed format for RPCPPE. The Inventory
Committee should also check the condition of the property, any damage, obsolete
and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and immediately request for disposal.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant should create, update and maintain the required
property cards and ledger cards. Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results of
the actual physical count with the property and accounting records. All
discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted
and reconciled immediately.

Furthermore, we reiterated that the physical count of PPE should be
undertaken annually by the Inventory Committee and the corresponding RPCPPE
be submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year as required
under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer - Designate to deposit immediately
all the cash collections under their custody with the authorized government
depository bank, in compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I. Likewise, they should submit an explanation letter for not depositing the
collections intact and promptly.

We also reiterated for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their respective
books to accounts. Any discrepancy noted should be investigated in accordance
with existing regulations and appropriate action should be taken thereon.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to stop the practice of
designating a Collector that is not a regular or permanent employee of the
Municipality, in compliance with of Section 305 (f) of Republic Act No. 7160 or the
Local Government Code.

We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Accountable
Forms Custodian to properly account and keep the AFs in a safety vault or a safety
cabinet to avoid loss, misuse or unauthorized use. Likewise, the Liquidating Officer
– Designate be provided with a safety vault for safekeeping of her collections.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to follow the
prescribed guidelines and procedures pertaining to Petty Cash Fund as stated under
Sections 48 and 52 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management should send
Demand Letters to those Accountable Officers to immediately settle their cash
advances with due recourse to the consequences that may be instituted as
enumerated above.

We also recommended for the Management to stop the practice of granting
advance payments or personal cash advances to officials and employees against
their salaries and allowances for a particular period which the services are not yet
rendered.

Likewise, we reiterated that the management should make other appropriate
action such as, but not limited to, institutions of sanctions as provided for under
Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

Henceforth, we recommended for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce
the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances
prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Office of the Municipal
Accountant periodically reconcile the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds,
Due to Other Funds and other reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these
accounts in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary
adjustments should be effected in the books of accounts.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to trace back the composition of Infrastructure Assets and prepare the
corresponding SLs.

We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory
Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to
the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended for the Management to procure
telephone/communications services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance with
the provisions of Appendix 13 of the IRR of the Government Procurement Reform
Act (RA 9184). Likewise, Officials and Employees to be granted by the Telephone
Expenses allocation/budget should be in line with Item No. 5, Annex D of COA
Circular No. 2012-003.

The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to require
that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be supported with
Statements of Account/Bill from telecommunication service providers, official
receipts/invoices and certification that calls are official as required in COA Circular
No. 2012-001. Likewise, official receipts/invoices printed in thermal papers should
be photocopied.

Moreover, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a rate
not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond just
measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into account the
prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication services
providers. The use/expenditure thereof not in accordance with RA 9184, pertinent
COA Circulars and other applicable laws, rules and regulations may be subject to
audit disallowance.

In addition, submit a copy of existing memorandum/order authorizing the
claim of Telephone Expenses and its corresponding rate per entitled
official/employee, or enact resolution for guidelines of telephone expenses.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should
instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and/or the
Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on
implementation of PPAs.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor through
its BAC to maintain the accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it
to the GPPB and to this Office within 14 days at the end of each semester.

We recommended that the BAC of the Municipality should invite COA
representative as Observer in its procurement process especially during
eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination
of bids, bid evaluation, opening of the bids, and post-qualification. Likewise, the
BAC should see to it that at least one observer (1) must come from a duly
recognized group in a sector and the other shall come from non-government
organization (NGO). The observers shall be invited at least five (5) calendar
days before the date of the procurement stage. In the event that a procurement
activity has to be postponed, the observers shall be notified immediately of the
change in schedule.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should require
their bidders to submit to Audit Team Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to
the HOPE or to the public officers or employees by consanguinity or affinity up
to the third civil degree and the same should be submitted to the Audit Team
together with the required documentary requirements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should procure
its common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) under Procurement
Service of DBM. If the BAC of the Municipality desires to procure its CSEs
other than the PS depot, the procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of
Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS).

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to reconstitute or amend the
BAC Composition to comply with Section 11.2.3 of R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipality should immediately submit to
us the requested documents to support its compliance with the pertinent law/s.
Non-compliance may result to suspension of the procurement concerned until
they have complied with the requirements.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with applicable laws
and rules of R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly the Bids
and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the the provisions of COA Circular
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. We also recommended the immediate
submission of the copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their
supporting documents which are due on the prescribed period to the Office of the
Auditor.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipality should pay for the delivered
items and accomplished projects only. Thus, it should avoid the advance
payment on the government contracts.

We recommended for the Municipality to enhance transparency in the
procurement especially by posting its procurement opportunities in the
PhilGEPS website, the website of the Municipality, if available, and at any
conspicuous place reserved for the purpose. Likewise, Section 21.2.1 of IRR of
R.A. No. 9184 must always be complied with.

We recommended that the Municipality, through its Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC), should require the submission of the eligibility
requirements such as Technical and Financial Documents as enumerated
under Section 23.1 of the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Likewise, the BAC should
award only the contract to the eligible supplier only.

We recommended that the document/s that must be submitted to the
Audit Team should be duly accomplished such as by proper dating, numbering
and signing.

We recommended that the process of preparing the Disbursement
Vouchers must conform with the proper procedures such as the pre-
requisites of gathering of the supporting documents and approved Obligation
Requests.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended the Municipality to submit the
documentary requirements timely, accurately and completely. Non-
submission would result to suspension or disallowance on our audit, as the
case may be.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with P.D. No. 1445,
COA Circular No. 2012-001, R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to fast
track the implementation of all the programmed projects and activities charged to
20% DF during the year and previous years to achieve effectively and efficiently its
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended the Municipality to faithfully comply with the
preparation and submission of the documents as prescribed under DEPED-
DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to deposit immediately the cash under Cash – Local Treasury account
in his custody with the authorized government depository bank, in compliance
with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. In case that the
Municipal Treasurer fails to deposit the cash under his custody, the Municipal
Mayor should make necessary action to compel the former of his duty.

We recommended that the disbursement to be made out of SEF must be
within the budget in accordance with Section 100(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and
Section 4 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Lastly, we recommended the Municipality, through its Municipal
Treasurer, to exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real
property for the Special Education Fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should allocate at
least 1% of its IRA for the LCPC in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344 and
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to ensure
implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor should establish the
Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC). Once established, the LCPC
should prepare plans, such as but not limited to: (1) Council’s Annual Work and
Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for the integration in the local
development plan, (2) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and (3) other
relevant plans for the allowable programs for the children under Section 15 of R.A.
No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. In
accordance with the said plans, the indicated programs, projects, and activities
should be properly disbursed and implemented with due regard to the existing
accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management should prepare and
submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. In addition,
we also recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on
Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal
Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Likewise, part of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of ₱14,579.98 for CY
2017 should be transferred to the Trust Fund and be deposited to the respective bank
account.

We further recommended that the guidelines provided in NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, COA Circular No. 2012-002
and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 for the
allocation, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF be strictly observed.

We reiterated our recommendations that management coodinate with
concerned agencies for the prompt approval and implementation of the Solid Waste
Management Plan of the municipality. We also recommended the management to
initiate the immediate establishment of Barangay Materials Recovery Facility.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to make necessary corrective
measures and devise a way to conform to the mentioned regulations.

Henceforth, we recommended the management to strictly comply with
provisions of RA No. 9003, otherwise known as the Ecological Solid Waste
Management Act of 2000 and its Implementing Rules and Regulation.

We recommended for the Management to strictly comply with Section 48 of
the General Provisions of R.A. No. 10964 known as the “General Appropriations Act
of FY 2018” for the monthly net take home pay of the regular employees for CY
2018 which is not lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00), after all authorized
deductions based on the above order of preference presented.

We recommended for the Management to deduct and remit the PhilHealth
contributions as prescribed by PhilHealth Circular No. 2017-0024 dated September
11, 2017, for CY 2018.

We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to remit the said contribution on the prescribed period to avoid interest and
surcharge, otherwise the Audit Team will issue a Notice of Charge (NC) for the
interests and surcharges incurred for delayed remittance.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant for the immediate remittance of taxes
withheld (all funds) to the BIR.

We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to adjust, trace and
resolve the discrepancy on the Due to BIR account balance and ensure that all
transactions are properly recorded.

Likewise, we recommended for the Municipal Treasurer to regularly check
the availability of accountable forms for all funds under his custody to avoid
borrowing of accountable forms of the other funds.

In addition, we recommended for the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that
supporting documents printed on thermal paper be photocopied to preserve the
contents of these documents.

Henceforth, we recommended for the Management to fully comply with the
provisions of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended by
RR Nos. 14-2002 dated September 9, 2002 and 10-2008 dated July 8, 2008 to avoid
possible interest/penalty for late payments and/or possible misuse of the government
funds.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
a. The Municipal Treasurer should explain why he should not be held administratively liable for not depositing his collection intact and promptly.

d. The Municipal Treasurer should regularly update his cashbooks.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended that the management should impose salary deductions from those officials and employees who have not settled their cash advances for travel or for other purposes, as well as the institution of other sanctions, as provided for under Sections 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 16 of Executive Order 248.
The Municipal Mayor should cause the strict implementation of the above recommendation as he is the one responsible for ensuring the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with the rules and regulations prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this report)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all changes in the annual budget such as re-appropriation of savings or re-programming of discontinued projects or abandonment of PPAs to any item of expenditure under any class or to any new item of expenditure not included in the annual budget shall be covered by an Appropriation Ordinance or Supplemental Budget. Submit the same to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.
We further recommended that any use of savings and augmentation of an existing item or expenditure within the same expense class shall be covered by ordinances authorizing the LCE and/or Presiding Officer of the Sanggunian in their respective appropriations.

We recommended strict adherence to PCW-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
The Municipal Mayor and the GAD Focal Person should identify gender issues in accordance with the aforementioned guidelines to ensure that GAD Fund allocation shall be utilized for PPAs that directly address gender disparities and promote gender equality, gender-responsive governance, women’s economic empowerment and protect and fulfill women’s human rights. The total disbursement for identified PPAs should be equivalent to the required fund allocation for GAD. Submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report in the prescribed format and within the deadline so that timely review and evaluation of the same is facilitated.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the budget for the ensuing years are prepared in accordance with the procedures, schedules and local budget preparation forms prescribed under the Budget Operations Manual for LGUs and for the Sangguniang Bayan to require, prior to the approval of the Annual and Supplemental Budgets, that special purpose appropriations are categorized by object of expenditure and presented in a similar sequence as a department or office expenditure program except the staffing and compensation profile where there are no personnel and office to operate.

We recommended that the BAC comply with the regulations and furnish the Auditor with the required copies of contract documents and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period. We also recommend that no item shall be left blank in accomplishing a purchase order. Furthermore, the Management thru its Bids and Award Committee is also requested to submit the required documents for all of the above transactions within the specified period.

Overall Recommendations
We recommended the Municipality to uphold and guide themselves with
the applicable laws and rules such as R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR, respectively.
Section 4(6) of PD 1445 states that “Claims against government funds shall
be supported with complete documentation.” In line with this, we recommend the
Municipality to submit accurately, timely and completely the documentary
requirements as prescribed by law, or rules and regulations. Section 39(3) of said
decree provides “It shall be the duty of the officials or employees concerned xxx
to comply promptly with these requirements. Failure or refusal to do so without
justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action
xxx.”

Lastly, no public officials or employees should be pecuniary interested,
directly or indirectly, in any contract or transaction of the government in
violation of the laws such as Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public
Officials and Employees (R.A. 6713) and Anti-Graft and Corrupt Practices Act
(R.A. 3019). Even if the public officials and employees of the Municipality don’t
know the law, they are still liable because Art. 3 of the Civil Code provides
“Ignorance of the law excuses no one from compliance therewith.”

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator / Acting Municipal
Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on
implementation of PPAs.

Henceforth, we recommend strict compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator / Acting Municipal Engineer to speed-
up the implementation of all the programmed projects and activities charged to
20% DF during the year and previous years to achieve effectively and efficiently
its socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

1. coodinate with concerned agencies for the prompt approval and
implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality;
2. include in the municipal programs an information campaign on proper
waste disposal to avoid hazardous effect of improper waste disposal to the
community and environment; and
3. initiate the immediate establishment of Municipal Sanitary Landfill and
Barangay Materials Recovery Facility.

We recommended that the local school board should prepare and enact
the local school board budget in accordance with the existing budgeting rules
and regulations.

Henceforth, the management should be guided and strictly complied with
the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 re:
Revised Guidelines on the Use of the Special Education Fund (SEF).

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and allocate at
least 1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344
and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to
ensure implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of
children.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management should prepare
and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. In
addition, we also recommend that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the

2016 should be transferred to the Trust Fund and be deposited to the respective
bank account. Henceforth, such transfer shall be made at the end of the year so

DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, COA Circular No.
2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1
for the allocation, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF be strictly observed.

We recommended the following corrective measures:
a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) should create an Inventory Committee, consisting of a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office.
b. The LCE should require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain ledger cards for each class and category of property and equipment indicating acquisition date, cost and the brief descriptions. For the purpose of reconciling the accounting records the reported balance of PPE per Balance Sheet should be used as the base amount of property per category.
c. Require the Treasurer’s Office to maintain Property Cards per class and category of property, plant and equipment indicating acquisition date, the brief descriptions and reconcile with the records of the accounting office to determine the correctness of the property records. Update the property issue slip/PAR to check custodianship of the property.
d. Require each department head to fast track inventory of all properties under their office and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification of the correctness and existence of the property.
e. The inventory committee should check the condition of the property, any damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable and Waste Material Report and immediately request for disposal. Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and furnish the accounting office for any adjustment and re-classification of property to other asset account.
f. Compel the inventory committee to fully accomplish the RPCPPE, indicating the actual count and complete details, and the results of the actual count shall be reconciled with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled immediately. From thereon, perform periodic inventory of PPE and prepare report on the results thereof, in accordance with existing regulations.
g. The Municipal Accountant cause the immediate transfer of the completed projects under the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and provide and record periodic depreciation for the depreciable assets under the General Fund to allow fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements. Furthermore, trace all the journal entries made affecting the Government Equity account under Trust Fund books in order to fully eliminate the said account.

In addition, we also recommend that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Separate bank account for LDRRMF was established and some funds were already transferred to that.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this report)

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of on-going government PPAs and those to be implemented during the year. The LCE should regularly submit the monitoring report and to submit at the beginning of the year a list of on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year. Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Report on PPAs was submitted.
observations.reason: However, it was delayed and it did not conform with the prescribed form.
